goals: Provide a ComfyUI node that accepts a VIDEO input and renders it as a playable, downloadable widget.
Use only existing dependencies (no new pip packages; plain browser JS/Canvas APIs).
Integrate seamlessly with ComfyUI's native VIDEO output type.

userStories.title: Node receives VIDEO input and displays it in a canvas widget
userStories.description: As an artist using ComfyUI, I want a node that accepts a VIDEO connection and renders the video frames inside a `<canvas>` widget embedded in the node so that I can preview the result without leaving the graph.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A new node `CoolVideoPlayer` is registered in `NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS` and `NODE_DISPLAY_NAME_MAPPINGS`.
The node declares a single input of type `VIDEO`.
The node's frontend widget renders a `<canvas>` element inside the node body.
When a VIDEO is connected and the graph is executed, the canvas displays the video frames.
Visually verified in browser: canvas appears inside the node with video content visible.

userStories.title: User can play and pause the video in the canvas widget
userStories.description: As an artist, I want play and pause controls on the canvas widget so that I can inspect specific frames of the generated video.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A play/pause toggle button is rendered below the canvas.
Clicking play starts frame-by-frame animation on the canvas; clicking pause stops it.
Visually verified in browser: play/pause toggle works correctly.

userStories.title: User can download the video from the node widget
userStories.description: As an artist, I want a download button on the canvas widget so that I can save the generated video to disk without leaving ComfyUI.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A "Download" button is rendered in the node widget alongside the canvas.
Clicking it triggers a browser download of the video file (preserving the original format served by ComfyUI).
The downloaded file opens and plays correctly in a standard video player.
Visually verified in browser: download button saves a valid video file.

functionalRequirements.description: The node class `CoolVideoPlayer` must be defined in `nodes/video_player.py` and registered in `__init__.py`.
The node input must use ComfyUI's native `VIDEO` type so it connects to any node that outputs VIDEO.
The node must pass the VIDEO reference through to the frontend (via `ui` output or ComfyUI's file-serving mechanism) without re-encoding or copying the file in Python.
The frontend widget must be implemented as a plain ES module in `web/video_player.js` — no bundler, no new npm dependencies.
The canvas widget must use the HTML5 `<canvas>` API and a `<video>` element as the frame source (draw video frames to canvas via `drawImage`).
The download link must point to the URL ComfyUI already uses to serve the VIDEO file (no custom HTTP endpoint required unless unavoidable).
The widget must resize the canvas to match the video's intrinsic width/height on load.
